About MLC

Built for Michigan.
Run by Michigan.

The Michigan Lean Consortium is a 501(c)(3) nonprofit, all-volunteer-governed organization founded in 2008 to advance continuous improvement across every industry in Michigan. Our founders' goal: make Michigan the best place in the country to live, learn, and earn.

Our Mission

Transform Michigan's organizations and economy by developing and supporting Lean Systems thinkers.

Our Vision

The primary resource in Michigan that supports, mentors, and encourages Lean Systems thinkers.

Our Values

Everything we do is guided by three core principles:

  • Respect for people
  • Inclusion of all learners at any level and with any experiences
  • Teaching and learning
Our Story

Making a positive difference
in Michigan's future

Founded in 2008, the Michigan Lean Consortium began with a simple idea: Michigan's organizations could achieve more by learning together. What started as a small group of Lean practitioners has grown into a statewide community that spans manufacturing, healthcare, education, government, and beyond.

Today, MLC connects hundreds of professionals through annual conferences, Gemba tours, virtual coffees, and hands-on workshops. We are volunteer-led and mission-driven, committed to making continuous improvement accessible to every corner of the state.

What Members Say
“Before MLC, we were trying to implement Lean in isolation. The conference and Gemba tours showed us what world-class looks like.”
— Director of Continuous Improvement, Spectrum Health
Our Team

The people behind the mission

MLC is a 501(c)(3) nonprofit governed by an all-volunteer Board of Directors, supported by a small dedicated staff and built on the vision of our founders.

Staff

Pamela Burgess

Executive Director & Board Chair

Pamela leads MLC's strategic vision and operations, working to expand the reach of Lean thinking across Michigan's diverse industries.

Lauren Ward

Operations & Membership

Lauren manages member relations, event coordination, and the daily operations that keep MLC's community connected and thriving.

MLC was founded in 2008 by a group of Lean practitioners who believed that if every Michigan organization used Lean principles, the state's economy would be unstoppable.
